California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornrnission
February 8, 199 L
Ms. coleen A. Scott
Treasurer, Mccandfiss for Mayor Committee
P. O. Box 1982
chula Vista, California 92012
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our FiIe No. I -9 o-7 08
Dear Ms. Scott:
This is in response to your request for assistance regarding
the Politicaf Reform Act (the 'Act")r and is in the nature of
inf orrnal- assistance. 2
After drafting our initial 
response to your request for
advice, we learned frorn you of the death of Mayor Gayle
Mccandliss, on whose behalf you had originally written us. The
advice letter previously prepared would be inappropriate in light
of the circumstances, and we consequently have framed our response
in consideration of the tasks you face as a committee treasurer
whose candidate has died. we realize that you are faced with
closing the llayor's conmittee accounts, and hope that this letter
will assist you in that rrork.
OUESTION
when an official 
leaves office before expiration of his or
her term, what would be appropriate uses of remaining canpaign
funds?
1 
co.r"rnment code Sections 81ooo-91015. AI1 statutory
references are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
Conmission regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations
Section 18o0o, et seo. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 
l.,fo.ra1 assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114,' Regulation 18329 (c) (3) . )
428 J street, suite 8oo . P.o. Box 8oz o sacramento cA 95go4-ogoz o (916) 3 zz-s66o


Our Fi■e No. A-90-708
Page 2
CONCLUSIONS
Funds remaining in either of Mayor Mccandliss′
 accounts
became :!surp■us funds'l upon the Mayor′
s deatho  As surp■
us funds′
the money in each account must be disposed of in accordance w■
th
Section 895■9.
FACTS
Former city councilmember Gayle Mccandliss won the Chu1a
Vista nayoral- contest in Novenber , l.99o I and subsequently died in
January, 1991. Prior to her death, you were concerned about the
propriety of transfering funds from a campaign account established
in connection with an earlier city council election and the
account established for the mayoral race. You were also concerned
about proper handling of funds in the event that the Mayor decided
to run for future office. 
The D1ayor's demise has eliminated any
need to answer your second concern, but one aspect of our previous
answer, i.e., disposition of surplus funds, continues to be
relevant, and thus is addressed belovr.
ANALYSI S
The funds rernaining in Mayor llccandliss' campaign accounts
are no!, surplus funds, and subject to the restrictions found in
section 89519, s/hich sets forth the following:
Upon leaving any elected office, or at the end
of the postelection reporting period following the
defeat of a candidate for elective office,
whichever occurs last, campaign funds raised after
January 1, 1989, under the control of the forrner
candidate or elected officer shal,I be considered
surplus carnpaign funds and shall be disclosed
pursuant to chapter 4 (commencing with Section
84100) and sha1l be used onfy for the following
purposes :
(a) The payment of outstanding canpaign debts
or elected officer's 
exPenses.
(b) The pro rata repal4rent of contributions.
(c) Donations to any bona fide charitable,
educational, civic, religious or sinilar tax-
exempt, nonprofit organization, where no
substantial part of the proceeds wiII have a
material financiaf effect on the former candidate
or elected offj-cer, any rnember of his or her
innediate farnily, or hj-s or her carnpaign treasurer.


Our Fi■e No. A-90-708
Page 3
(d) Contributions to a political party or
committee so long as the funds are not used to make
contributions in support of or opposition to a
candidate for elective office.
(e) contributions to support or oppose any
candidate for federal office, any candidate for
el,ective office in a state other than California,
or any ballot measure.
You have indicated to me in telephone conversations that
there is a non-profit organi.zation in which the I'layor had a
special interest and to which you wouLd Like to give the surplus
funds. rf that organization fits the description set forth in
Section 89519 (c) , there should be no problem under the Act in
donating funds to that group.
I trust that the above answers your guestions. If you need
further information, please do not hesitate to contact ne at (915)
322-590L.
S incerely,
scott
Acting
Ha I Iabr in
General counsel
susan L.
CounseI, Legal Division
SH:SLB:ken
